response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: Picked three rounds far apart and checked the two internal consistency equations that would instantly expose a 1-bit fault.

1. Round 0  
   • step1 = h + S1  (L0070,L0072)  
     0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x9167F444 – matches the value at L0072.  
   • step2 = step1 + ch  (L0071,L0073)  
     0x9167F444 + 0x1F85C98C = 0xB0EDBDD0 – matches L0073.  

2. Round 45  
   • new e = d + temp1  (L0705,L0710)  
     d = 0xC766213B (from L0699)  
     temp1 = 0xC28097BC (L0705)  
     Sum = 0x89E6B8F7 – exactly the “new e” at L0710.  

3. Round 47  
   • new e = d + temp1  (L0733,L0738)  
     d = 0x41F6D567 (L0727)  
     temp1 = 0x033845FF (L0733)  
     Sum = 0x452F1B66 – matches L0738.  
   • new a = temp1 + temp2  (L0733,L0736,L0737)  
     temp2 = 0x3B B6 4 3E? (L0736) ; sum reproduced as 0x3E EE 90 3D and equals “new a” at L0737.

Since all these independent calculations agree with the trace and only one bit could have been tampered, the trace must be internally perfect – therefore genuine.
